revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Onsen Jinja (温泉神社) |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |image = [[File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg|280px|]]
Haiden (拝殿) |location = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Iwaki|ja|いわき市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki|qq=}} 常磐湯本町三函322 |Latitude_Degrees = 37 |Latitude_Minutes = 0 |Latitude_Seconds = 30 |Longitude_Degrees = 140 |Longitude_Minutes = 50 |Longitude_Seconds = 43 |Label_Position = left |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} |Sacred_Object = |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Founded = {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|673|ja|673|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/673|qq=}}) |Main_Hall = {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irimoya-zukuri|qq=}} |Alternate_Name = Sabako Shrine (佐波古神社) |Sadosho = |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |festival = {{ill|May 2|ja|5月2日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 2|qq=}} |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''Onsen Jinja''' (温泉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Iwaki|ja|いわき市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki|qq=}}. It is a small {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} it was designated as a prefectural shrine. It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|qq=}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|673|ja|673|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/673|qq=}}) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|qq=}} 3 ({{ill|1340|ja|1340|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1340|qq=}}) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiwa|qq=}} 5 ({{ill|1768|ja|1768|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1768|qq=}}) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|承和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} 5 ({{ill|863|ja|863|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/863|qq=}}) on {{ill|October 29|ja|10月29日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 29|qq=}}, it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ({{ill|Shintaizan|ja|神体山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintaizan|qq=}}) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku|qq=}} 8 ({{ill|1695|ja|1695|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1695|qq=}}) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}} {{ill|Joban Line|ja|常磐線|1=Jōban Line|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joban Line|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōban Line|qq=}} {{ill|Yumoto Station|ja|湯本駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Station|qq=}} == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|qq=}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]][[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] [[sn:Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|承和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} 5 ({{ill|863|ja|863|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/863|qq=}}) on {{ill|October 29|ja|10月29日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 29|qq=}}, it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ({{ill|Shintaizan|ja|神体山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintaizan|qq=}}) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku|qq=}} 8 ({{ill|1695|ja|1695|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1695|qq=}}) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}} {{ill|Joban Line|ja|常磐線|1=Jōban Line|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joban Line|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōban Line|qq=}} {{ill|Yumoto Station|ja|湯本駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Station|qq=}} == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|qq=}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] [[sn:Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Shrine |Name = Onsen Jinja (温泉神社) |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |image = [[File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg|280px|]]
Haiden (拝殿) |location = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Iwaki|ja|いわき市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki|qq=}} 常磐湯本町三函322 |Latitude_Degrees = 37 |Latitude_Minutes = 0 |Latitude_Seconds = 30 |Longitude_Degrees = 140 |Longitude_Minutes = 50 |Longitude_Seconds = 43 |Label_Position = left |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} |Sacred_Object = |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Founded = {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|673|ja|673|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/673|qq=}}) |Main_Hall = {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irimoya-zukuri|qq=}} |Alternate_Name = Sabako Shrine (佐波古神社) |Sadosho = |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |festival = {{ill|May 2|ja|5月2日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 2|qq=}} |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''Onsen Jinja''' (温泉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Iwaki|ja|いわき市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki|qq=}}. It is a small {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} it was designated as a prefectural shrine. It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|qq=}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|673|ja|673|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/673|qq=}}) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|qq=}} 3 ({{ill|1340|ja|1340|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1340|qq=}}) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in {{ill|Meiwa|ja|明和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiwa|qq=}} 5 ({{ill|1768|ja|1768|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1768|qq=}}) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|承和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} 5 ({{ill|863|ja|863|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/863|qq=}}) on {{ill|October 29|ja|10月29日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 29|qq=}}, it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ({{ill|Shintaizan|ja|神体山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintaizan|qq=}}) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku|qq=}} 8 ({{ill|1695|ja|1695|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1695|qq=}}) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}} {{ill|Joban Line|ja|常磐線|1=Jōban Line|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joban Line|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōban Line|qq=}} {{ill|Yumoto Station|ja|湯本駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Station|qq=}} == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|qq=}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] [[sn:Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Founded = {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|673|ja|673|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/673|qq=}}) |Main_Hall = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] |Alternate_Name = Sabako Shrine (佐波古神社) |Sadosho = |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |festival = [[:en:May 2|May 2]] |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''Onsen Jinja''' (温泉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki]]. It is a small {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] it was designated as a prefectural shrine. It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|qq=}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|673|ja|673|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/673|qq=}}) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|qq=}} 3 ({{ill|1340|ja|1340|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1340|qq=}}) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 5 ({{ill|1768|ja|1768|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1768|qq=}}) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In [[:en:Jōwa|Jōgan]] 5 ({{ill|863|ja|863|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/863|qq=}}) on [[:en:October 29|October 29]], it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ([[:en:Sacred mountains|Shintaizan]]) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ({{ill|1695|ja|1695|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1695|qq=}}) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10‑minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] [[:en:Yumoto Station|Yumoto Station]] == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|qq=}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] [[sn:Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|qq=}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|673|ja|673|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/673|qq=}}) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|qq=}} 3 ({{ill|1340|ja|1340|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1340|qq=}}) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 5 ({{ill|1768|ja|1768|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1768|qq=}}) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In [[:en:Jōwa|Jōgan]] 5 ({{ill|863|ja|863|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/863|qq=}}) on [[:en:October 29|October 29]], it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ([[:en:Sacred mountains|Shintaizan]]) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ({{ill|1695|ja|1695|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1695|qq=}}) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10‑minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] [[:en:Yumoto Station|Yumoto Station]] == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|qq=}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] [[sn:Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]]

In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|qq=}} 3 ({{ill|1340|ja|1340|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1340|qq=}}) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 5 ({{ill|1768|ja|1768|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1768|qq=}}) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In [[:en:Jōwa|Jōgan]] 5 ({{ill|863|ja|863|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/863|qq=}}) on [[:en:October 29|October 29]], it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ([[:en:Sacred mountains|Shintaizan]]) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ({{ill|1695|ja|1695|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1695|qq=}}) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} |Sacred_Object = |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |Founded = {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|673|ja|673|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/673|qq=}}) |Main_Hall = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] |Alternate_Name = Sabako Shrine (佐波古神社) |Sadosho = |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |festival = [[:en:May 2|May 2]] |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''Onsen Jinja''' (温泉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki]]. It is a small {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] it was designated as a prefectural shrine. It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|qq=}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|673|ja|673|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/673|qq=}}) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|qq=}} 3 ({{ill|1340|ja|1340|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1340|qq=}}) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 5 ({{ill|1768|ja|1768|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1768|qq=}}) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In [[:en:Jōwa|Jōgan]] 5 ({{ill|863|ja|863|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/863|qq=}}) on [[:en:October 29|October 29]], it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ([[:en:Sacred mountains|Shintaizan]]) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ({{ill|1695|ja|1695|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1695|qq=}}) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ({{ill|1340|ja|1340|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1340|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1000}}) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 5 ({{ill|1768|ja|1768|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1768|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In [[:en:Jōwa|Jōgan]] 5 ({{ill|863|ja|863|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/863|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) on [[:en:October 29|October 29]], it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ([[:en:Sacred mountains|Shintaizan]]) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ({{ill|1695|ja|1695|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1695|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. It is a small {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] it was designated as a prefectural shrine. It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|qq=}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ({{ill|1340|ja|1340|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1340|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1000}}) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 5 ({{ill|1768|ja|1768|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1768|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) it was relocated to its present location. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ({{ill|1340|ja|1340|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1340|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1000}}) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 5 ({{ill|1768|ja|1768|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1768|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In [[:en:Jōwa|Jōgan]] 5 ({{ill|863|ja|863|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/863|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) on [[:en:October 29|October 29]], it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ([[:en:Sacred mountains|Shintaizan]]) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ({{ill|1695|ja|1695|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1695|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10‑minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] [[:en:Yumoto Station|Yumoto Station]] == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)]] [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] [[sn:Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}}

It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|673|ja|673|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/673|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 600}}) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|673|ja|673|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/673|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 600}}) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ([[:en:1340|1340]]) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 5 ({{ill|1768|ja|1768|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1768|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In [[:en:Jōwa|Jōgan]] 5 ({{ill|863|ja|863|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/863|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) on [[:en:October 29|October 29]], it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ([[:en:Sacred mountains|Shintaizan]]) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ([[:en:1695|1695]]) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10‑minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] [[:en:Yumoto Station|Yumoto Station]] == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)]] [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] [[sn:Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}}

In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ([[:en:1340|1340]]) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 5 ([[:en:1768|1768]]) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In [[:en:Jōwa|Jōgan]] 5 ([[:en:863|863]]) on [[:en:October 29|October 29]], it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ([[:en:Sacred mountains|Shintaizan]]) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ([[:en:1695|1695]]) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10‑minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] [[:en:Yumoto Station|Yumoto Station]] == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)]] [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] [[sn:Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}}

It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|qq=}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=}} 2 ([[:en:673|673]]) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ([[:en:1340|1340]]) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 5 ([[:en:1768|1768]]) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In [[:en:Jōwa|Jōgan]] 5 ([[:en:863|863]]) on [[:en:October 29|October 29]], it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ([[:en:Sacred mountains|Shintaizan]]) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ([[:en:1695|1695]]) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10‑minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] [[:en:Yumoto Station|Yumoto Station]] == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)]] [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] [[sn:Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}}

It is a small {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] it was designated as a prefectural shrine. It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|qq=|1=Yumoto Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563581}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ([[:en:673|673]]) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ([[:en:1340|1340]]) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 5 ([[:en:1768|1768]]) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In [[:en:Jōwa|Jōgan]] 5 ([[:en:863|863]]) on [[:en:October 29|October 29]], it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ([[:en:Sacred mountains|Shintaizan]]) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ([[:en:1695|1695]]) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10‑minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] [[:en:Yumoto Station|Yumoto Station]] == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)]] [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] [[sn:Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} {{wikidata link|Q54153253}}

{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} |Sacred_Object = |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founded = {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ([[:en:673|673]]) |Main_Hall = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] |Alternate_Name = Sabako Shrine (佐波古神社) |Sadosho = |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |festival = [[:en:May 2|May 2]] |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''Onsen Jinja''' (温泉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki]]. It is a small {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] it was designated as a prefectural shrine. It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|qq=|1=Yumoto Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563581}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|qq=|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ([[:en:673|673]]) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ([[:en:1340|1340]]) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 5 ([[:en:1768|1768]]) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In [[:en:Jōwa|Jōgan]] 5 ([[:en:863|863]]) on [[:en:October 29|October 29]], it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ([[:en:Sacred mountains|Shintaizan]]) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ([[:en:1695|1695]]) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10‑minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] [[:en:Yumoto Station|Yumoto Station]] == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} {{wikidata link|Q54153253}}

Haiden (拝殿) |location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]][[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki]] 常磐湯本町三函322 |Latitude_Degrees = 37 |Latitude_Minutes = 0 |Latitude_Seconds = 30 |Longitude_Degrees = 140 |Longitude_Minutes = 50 |Longitude_Seconds = 43 |Label_Position = left |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} |Sacred_Object = |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founded = {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ([[:en:673|673]]) |Main_Hall = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] |Alternate_Name = Sabako Shrine (佐波古神社) |Sadosho = |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |festival = [[:en:May 2|May 2]] |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''Onsen Jinja''' (温泉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki]]. It is a small {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] it was designated as a prefectural shrine. It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|1=Yumoto Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563581}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ([[:en:673|673]]) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ([[:en:1340|1340]]) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 5 ([[:en:1768|1768]]) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In [[:en:Jōwa|Jōgan]] 5 ([[:en:863|863]]) on [[:en:October 29|October 29]], it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ([[:en:Sacred mountains|Shintaizan]]) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ([[:en:1695|1695]]) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10‑minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] [[:en:Yumoto Station|Yumoto Station]] == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} {{wikidata link|Q54153253}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)]][[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q54153253]] Wikidata name: Onsen Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Onsen Jinja (温泉神社) |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image = [[File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg|280px|]]
Haiden (拝殿) |location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]][[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki]] 常磐湯本町三函322 |Latitude_Degrees = 37 |Latitude_Minutes = 0 |Latitude_Seconds = 30 |Longitude_Degrees = 140 |Longitude_Minutes = 50 |Longitude_Seconds = 43 |Label_Position = left |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} |Sacred_Object = |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founded = {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ([[:en:673|673]]) |Main_Hall = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] |Alternate_Name = Sabako Shrine (佐波古神社) |Sadosho = |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |festival = [[:en:May 2|May 2]] |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''Onsen Jinja''' (温泉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki]]. It is a small {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] it was designated as a prefectural shrine. It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|1=Yumoto Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563581}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ([[:en:673|673]]) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ([[:en:1340|1340]]) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 5 ([[:en:1768|1768]]) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In [[:en:Jōwa|Jōgan]] 5 ([[:en:863|863]]) on [[:en:October 29|October 29]], it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ([[:en:Sacred mountains|Shintaizan]]) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ([[:en:1695|1695]]) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10‑minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] [[:en:Yumoto Station|Yumoto Station]] == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} {{wikidata link|Q54153253}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)]][[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q54153253]] Wikidata name: Onsen Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Onsen Jinja (温泉神社) |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image = [[File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg|280px|]]
Haiden (拝殿) |location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]][[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki]] 常磐湯本町三函322 |Latitude_Degrees = 37 |Latitude_Minutes = 0 |Latitude_Seconds = 30 |Longitude_Degrees = 140 |Longitude_Minutes = 50 |Longitude_Seconds = 43 |Label_Position = left |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} |Sacred_Object = |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founded = {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ([[:en:673|673]]) |Main_Hall = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] |Alternate_Name = Sabako Shrine (佐波古神社) |Sadosho = |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |festival = [[:en:May 2|May 2]] |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''Onsen Jinja''' (温泉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki]]. It is a small {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] it was designated as a prefectural shrine. It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|1=Yumoto Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563581}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ([[:en:673|673]]) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ([[:en:1340|1340]]) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 5 ([[:en:1768|1768]]) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In [[:en:Jōwa|Jōgan]] 5 ([[:en:863|863]]) on [[:en:October 29|October 29]], it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ([[:en:Sacred mountains|Shintaizan]]) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ([[:en:1695|1695]]) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10‑minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] [[:en:Yumoto Station|Yumoto Station]] == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} {{wikidata link|Q54153253}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In [[:en:Jōwa|Jōgan]] 5 ([[:en:863|863]]) on [[:en:October 29|October 29]], it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ([[:en:Sacred mountains|Shintaizan]]) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ([[:en:1695|1695]]) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10‑minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] [[:en:Yumoto Station|Yumoto Station]] == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} {{wikidata link|Q54153253}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]
