revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)]][[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q54153253]] Wikidata name: Onsen Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Onsen Jinja (温泉神社) |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image = [[File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg|280px|]]
Haiden (拝殿) |location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]][[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki]] 常磐湯本町三函322 |Latitude_Degrees = 37 |Latitude_Minutes = 0 |Latitude_Seconds = 30 |Longitude_Degrees = 140 |Longitude_Minutes = 50 |Longitude_Seconds = 43 |Label_Position = left |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} |Sacred_Object = |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founded = {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ([[:en:673|673]]) |Main_Hall = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] |Alternate_Name = Sabako Shrine (佐波古神社) |Sadosho = |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |festival = [[:en:May 2|May 2]] |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''Onsen Jinja''' (温泉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki]]. It is a small {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] it was designated as a prefectural shrine. It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|1=Yumoto Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563581}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ([[:en:673|673]]) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ([[:en:1340|1340]]) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 5 ([[:en:1768|1768]]) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In [[:en:Jōwa|Jōgan]] 5 ([[:en:863|863]]) on [[:en:October 29|October 29]], it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ([[:en:Sacred mountains|Shintaizan]]) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ([[:en:1695|1695]]) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10‑minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] [[:en:Yumoto Station|Yumoto Station]] == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} {{wikidata link|Q54153253}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In [[:en:Jōwa|Jōgan]] 5 ([[:en:863|863]]) on [[:en:October 29|October 29]], it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ([[:en:Sacred mountains|Shintaizan]]) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ([[:en:1695|1695]]) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10‑minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] [[:en:Yumoto Station|Yumoto Station]] == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} {{wikidata link|Q54153253}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Haiden (拝殿) |location = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]][[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki]] 常磐湯本町三函322 |Latitude_Degrees = 37 |Latitude_Minutes = 0 |Latitude_Seconds = 30 |Longitude_Degrees = 140 |Longitude_Minutes = 50 |Longitude_Seconds = 43 |Label_Position = left |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} |Sacred_Object = |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founded = {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ([[:en:673|673]]) |Main_Hall = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] |Alternate_Name = Sabako Shrine (佐波古神社) |Sadosho = |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |festival = [[:en:May 2|May 2]] |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''Onsen Jinja''' (温泉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki]]. It is a small {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] it was designated as a prefectural shrine. It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|1=Yumoto Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563581}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ([[:en:673|673]]) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ([[:en:1340|1340]]) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 5 ([[:en:1768|1768]]) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In [[:en:Jōwa|Jōgan]] 5 ([[:en:863|863]]) on [[:en:October 29|October 29]], it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ([[:en:Sacred mountains|Shintaizan]]) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ([[:en:1695|1695]]) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10‑minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] [[:en:Yumoto Station|Yumoto Station]] == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} {{wikidata link|Q54153253}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It is a small {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] it was designated as a prefectural shrine. It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|1=Yumoto Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563581}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ([[:en:673|673]]) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ([[:en:1340|1340]]) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 5 ([[:en:1768|1768]]) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In [[:en:Jōwa|Jōgan]] 5 ([[:en:863|863]]) on [[:en:October 29|October 29]], it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ([[:en:Sacred mountains|Shintaizan]]) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 8 ([[:en:1695|1695]]) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10‑minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]] [[:en:Yumoto Station|Yumoto Station]] == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} {{wikidata link|Q54153253}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|1=Yumoto Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563581}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ([[:en:1340|1340]]) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa]] 5 ([[:en:1768|1768]]) it was relocated to its present location. {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} |Sacred_Object = |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founded = {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ({{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673}}) |Main_Hall = {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri}} |Alternate_Name = Sabako Shrine (佐波古神社) |Sadosho = |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |festival = {{ill|May 2|en|May 2|WD=Q2545|lt=May 2}} |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''Onsen Jinja''' (温泉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine}} located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki}}. It is a small {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was designated as a prefectural shrine. It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|1=Yumoto Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563581}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ({{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673}}) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ({{ill|1340|en|1340|WD=Q6056|lt=1340}}) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa}} 5 ({{ill|1768|en|1768|WD=Q7675|lt=1768}}) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In {{ill|Jōwa|en|Jōwa|WD=Q3577279|lt=Jōgan}} 5 ({{ill|863|en|863|WD=Q25052|lt=863}}) on {{ill|October 29|en|October 29|WD=Q2951|lt=October 29}}, it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ({{ill|Sacred mountains|en|Sacred mountains|WD=Q1595289|lt=Shintaizan}}) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 8 ({{ill|1695|en|1695|WD=Q7696|lt=1695}}) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10‑minute walk from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East}} {{ill|Jōban Line|en|Jōban Line|WD=Q1369263|lt=Jōban Line}} {{ill|Yumoto Station|en|Yumoto Station|WD=Q5353354|lt=Yumoto Station}} == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} {{wikidata link|Q54153253}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|1=Yumoto Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563581}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ({{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673}}) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ({{ill|1340|en|1340|WD=Q6056|lt=1340}}) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa}} 5 ({{ill|1768|en|1768|WD=Q7675|lt=1768}}) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In {{ill|Jōwa|en|Jōwa|WD=Q3577279|lt=Jōgan}} 5 ({{ill|863|en|863|WD=Q25052|lt=863}}) on {{ill|October 29|en|October 29|WD=Q2951|lt=October 29}}, it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ({{ill|Sacred mountains|en|Sacred mountains|WD=Q1595289|lt=Shintaizan}}) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 8 ({{ill|1695|en|1695|WD=Q7696|lt=1695}}) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10‑minute walk from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East}} {{ill|Jōban Line|en|Jōban Line|WD=Q1369263|lt=Jōban Line}} {{ill|Yumoto Station|en|Yumoto Station|WD=Q5353354|lt=Yumoto Station}} == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} {{wikidata link|Q54153253}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} |Sacred_Object = |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founded = {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ({{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673}}) |Main_Hall = {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri}} |Alternate_Name = Sabako Shrine (佐波古神社) |Sadosho = |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |festival = {{ill|May 2|en|May 2|WD=Q2545|lt=May 2}} |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''Onsen Jinja''' (温泉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine}} located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki}}. It is a small {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was designated as a prefectural shrine. It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|1=Yumoto Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563581}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ({{ill|1340|en|1340|WD=Q6056|lt=1340}}) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa}} 5 ({{ill|1768|en|1768|WD=Q7675|lt=1768}}) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In {{ill|Jōwa|en|Jōwa|WD=Q3577279|lt=Jōgan}} 5 ({{ill|863|en|863|WD=Q25052|lt=863}}) on {{ill|October 29|en|October 29|WD=Q2951|lt=October 29}}, it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} |Sacred_Object = |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founded = {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ({{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673}}) |Main_Hall = {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri}} |Alternate_Name = Sabako Shrine (佐波古神社) |Sadosho = |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |festival = {{ill|May 2|en|May 2|WD=Q2545|lt=May 2}} |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''Onsen Jinja''' (温泉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine}} located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki}}. It is a small {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was designated as a prefectural shrine. It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|1=Yumoto Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563581}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ({{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673}}) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ({{ill|1340|en|1340|WD=Q6056|lt=1340}}) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa}} 5 ({{ill|1768|en|1768|WD=Q7675|lt=1768}}) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In {{ill|Jōwa|en|Jōwa|WD=Q3577279|lt=Jōgan}} 5 ({{ill|863|en|863|WD=Q25052|lt=863}}) on {{ill|October 29|en|October 29|WD=Q2951|lt=October 29}}, it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ({{ill|Sacred mountains|en|Sacred mountains|WD=Q1595289|lt=Shintaizan}}) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 8 ({{ill|1695|en|1695|WD=Q7696|lt=1695}}) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10‑minute walk from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East}} {{ill|Jōban Line|en|Jōban Line|WD=Q1369263|lt=Jōban Line}} {{ill|Yumoto Station|en|Yumoto Station|WD=Q5353354|lt=Yumoto Station}} == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} {{wikidata link|Q54153253}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ({{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673}}) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ({{ill|1340|en|1340|WD=Q6056|lt=1340}}) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa}} 5 ({{ill|1768|en|1768|WD=Q7675|lt=1768}}) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In {{ill|Jōwa|en|Jōwa|WD=Q3577279|lt=Jōgan}} 5 ({{ill|863|en|863|WD=Q25052|lt=863}}) on {{ill|October 29|en|October 29|WD=Q2951|lt=October 29}}, it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ({{ill|Sacred mountains|en|Sacred mountains|WD=Q1595289|lt=Shintaizan}}) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 8 ({{ill|1695|en|1695|WD=Q7696|lt=1695}}) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East}} {{ill|Jōban Line|en|Jōban Line|WD=Q1369263|lt=Jōban Line}} {{ill|Yumoto Station|en|Yumoto Station|WD=Q5353354|lt=Yumoto Station}} == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} {{wikidata link|Q54153253}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

Haiden (拝殿) |location = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}} 常磐湯本町三函322 |Latitude_Degrees = 37 |Latitude_Minutes = 0 |Latitude_Seconds = 30 |Longitude_Degrees = 140 |Longitude_Minutes = 50 |Longitude_Seconds = 43 |Label_Position = left |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} |Sacred_Object = |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founded = {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ({{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673|1=673}}) |Main_Hall = {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} |Alternate_Name = Sabako Shrine (佐波古神社) |Sadosho = |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |festival = {{ill|May 2|en|May 2|WD=Q2545|lt=May 2|1=May 2}} |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''Onsen Jinja''' (温泉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}. It is a small {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was designated as a prefectural shrine. It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|1=Q11563581|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563581}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ({{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673|1=673}}) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ({{ill|1340|en|1340|WD=Q6056|lt=1340|1=1340}}) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa|1=Meiwa}} 5 ({{ill|1768|en|1768|WD=Q7675|lt=1768|1=1768}}) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In {{ill|Jōwa|en|Jōwa|WD=Q3577279|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōwa}} 5 ({{ill|863|en|863|WD=Q25052|lt=863|1=863}}) on {{ill|October 29|en|October 29|WD=Q2951|lt=October 29|1=October 29}}, it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ({{ill|Sacred mountains|en|Sacred mountains|WD=Q1595289|lt=Shintaizan|1=Sacred mountains}}) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 8 ({{ill|1695|en|1695|WD=Q7696|lt=1695|1=1695}}) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Jōban Line|en|Jōban Line|WD=Q1369263|lt=Jōban Line|1=Jōban Line}} {{ill|Yumoto Station|en|Yumoto Station|WD=Q5353354|lt=Yumoto Station|1=Yumoto Station}} == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} {{wikidata link|Q54153253}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)]][[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)]][[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)]][[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Onsen Jinja (温泉神社) |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |image = [[File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg|280px|]]
Haiden (拝殿) |location = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}} 常磐湯本町三函322 |Latitude_Degrees = 37 |Latitude_Minutes = 0 |Latitude_Seconds = 30 |Longitude_Degrees = 140 |Longitude_Minutes = 50 |Longitude_Seconds = 43 |Label_Position = left |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} |Sacred_Object = |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founded = {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ({{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673|1=673}}) |Main_Hall = {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} |Alternate_Name = Sabako Shrine (佐波古神社) |Sadosho = |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |festival = {{ill|May 2|en|May 2|WD=Q2545|lt=May 2|1=May 2}} |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''Onsen Jinja''' (温泉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}. It is a small {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was designated as a prefectural shrine. It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|1=Q11563581|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563581}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ({{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673|1=673}}) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ({{ill|1340|en|1340|WD=Q6056|lt=1340|1=1340}}) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa|1=Meiwa}} 5 ({{ill|1768|en|1768|WD=Q7675|lt=1768|1=1768}}) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In {{ill|Jōwa|en|Jōwa|WD=Q3577279|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōwa}} 5 ({{ill|863|en|863|WD=Q25052|lt=863|1=863}}) on {{ill|October 29|en|October 29|WD=Q2951|lt=October 29|1=October 29}}, it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ({{ill|Sacred mountains|en|Sacred mountains|WD=Q1595289|lt=Shintaizan|1=Sacred mountains}}) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 8 ({{ill|1695|en|1695|WD=Q7696|lt=1695|1=1695}}) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Jōban Line|en|Jōban Line|WD=Q1369263|lt=Jōban Line|1=Jōban Line}} {{ill|Yumoto Station|en|Yumoto Station|WD=Q5353354|lt=Yumoto Station|1=Yumoto Station}} == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} {{wikidata link|Q54153253}} [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founded = {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|zh|白鳳|ru|Хакухо|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ({{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673|1=673}}) |Main_Hall = {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} |Alternate_Name = Sabako Shrine (佐波古神社) |Sadosho = |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |festival = {{ill|May 2|en|May 2|WD=Q2545|lt=May 2|1=May 2}} |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} [[File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''Onsen Jinja''' (温泉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}. It is a small {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was designated as a prefectural shrine. It serves as the guardian shrine of {{ill|Yumoto Onsen|ja|湯本温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoto Onsen|1=Q11563581|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563581}}. == History == === Overview === According to the shrine tradition, the shrine was originally enshrined at Yunodake (Sabako Peak, 三箱山), located 5 km northwest of the present site. It is said that in {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} 2 ({{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673|1=673}}) the first priest, 小子部宿禰佐波古直足, transferred the enshrinement to Yumoto Sanhako, marking the establishment of the shrine. In the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} it was classified as a small shrine. In {{ill|Ryakuō|ja|良応|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryakuō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 ({{ill|1340|en|1340|WD=Q6056|lt=1340|1=1340}}) it was moved to Kannon-yama (located to the north of the current site), and later in {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa|1=Meiwa}} 5 ({{ill|1768|en|1768|WD=Q7675|lt=1768|1=1768}}) it was relocated to its present location. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a prefectural shrine. === Divine Rank === * In {{ill|Jōwa|en|Jōwa|WD=Q3577279|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōwa}} 5 ({{ill|863|en|863|WD=Q25052|lt=863|1=863}}) on {{ill|October 29|en|October 29|WD=Q2951|lt=October 29|1=October 29}}, it received the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (as recorded in the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}''). === Shrine Clergy === From ancient times, '''Sabako-shi''' (佐波古氏) has continuously held the hereditary position of shrine priest. The origin of the shrine’s alternate name, Sabako Shrine, is attributed to the fact that the shrine enshrines Yunodake (Sabako Peak) as its sacred mountain ({{ill|Sacred mountains|en|Sacred mountains|WD=Q1595289|lt=Shintaizan|1=Sacred mountains}}) and the hereditary priestly family is the Sabako-shi. == Shrine Facilities == The main hall was constructed in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 8 ({{ill|1695|en|1695|WD=Q7696|lt=1695|1=1695}}) and has been designated as a tangible cultural property by Iwaki City. File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura Hall File:Tokiwaki Inari Jinja.jpg|Subsidiary Shrine: Tokiwaki Inari Shrine File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 6.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 7.jpg|Shrine Building File:Onsen Jinja Iwaki 8.jpg|Sacred Bath == Transportation Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Jōban Line|en|Jōban Line|WD=Q1369263|lt=Jōban Line|1=Jōban Line}} {{ill|Yumoto Station|en|Yumoto Station|WD=Q5353354|lt=Yumoto Station|1=Yumoto Station}} == References == {{No footnotes|date=2020th year 3月|section=1}} * Historical Record * Shrine Head Notice Board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Commons|ページ名=Category:Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} == External Links == * [http://onsen-jinja.or.jp/ Onsen Jinja (温泉神社)] (Official website) {{Shinto navigation}} {{draft categories| [[ja:温泉神社 (いわき市)]] }} [[ja:温泉神社]] {{translated page|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|version=103716103|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Onsen-jinja (Iwaki)}} {{wikidata link|Q54153253}} [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
